I have visited Mexico City.
I visited Mexico City last year.
Have you ever climbed a pyramid?
We went to the market yesterday.
Mateo, I am so excited. I have never been to Mexico City. You grew up there, so what was your favorite neighborhood?
I grew up in Coyoacan, a calm but bustling neighborhood. I have lived in many places, but I still love it the most.
That sounds perfect. Have you ever visited the ancient ruins at Teotihuacan?
Many times. Last year I took my cousins there, and climbing the old pyramids was the highlight of the day.
Amazing. I have already booked a hotel, but I have not planned my food stops yet. Where did you eat?
Go to the markets. They are crowded but affordable. Take a slow stroll and try everything. You will have an unforgettable trip.
Have you ever been to Mexico City?
I have already planned my itinerary.
Last year I visited the ancient ruins.
What was the highlight of your trip?
I have always wanted to see the murals.

Hi Tuca, it is Daniel. So you are finally planning your trip to Mexico City, that is wonderful. I have been there three times, and I love it more every visit. Let me give you a few tips. The city is huge and bustling, but do not worry. Last year I stayed in a neighborhood called Roma Norte, and it was perfect: full of small cafes, colorful murals, and friendly street vendors. I have already visited the famous ancient ruins at Teotihuacan, and climbing the old pyramids was the highlight of my whole trip. The historic center gets very crowded on weekends, so try to go early in the morning. The food is amazing and surprisingly affordable, so take a slow stroll and eat at the local markets. One more thing: I have never felt unsafe there, but always keep your eyes open like in any big city. Have an unforgettable trip, and send me lots of photos.

Hey, it is Tuca again. I just finished planning my itinerary for Mexico City and I am so excited. I have never traveled to Mexico before, so this is a big adventure for me. Daniel sent me some wonderful advice. I have already booked a small hotel in a quiet neighborhood, and I made a day-by-day plan. On the first day I want to do some sightseeing in the historic center and see the huge murals. I have always wanted to visit the ancient ruins outside the city, so on day two I will take a tour to the pyramids. Daniel told me the markets are very affordable, so I added a long stroll through them to taste the local food. I read that some areas get really crowded, so I planned to start early every morning. I have not finished every detail yet, but my itinerary already looks unforgettable.

Tuca dreamed of visiting Mexico City for years. First, she called her friend Daniel, who has traveled there many times. Then she booked a small hotel in a quiet neighborhood. She made a day-by-day itinerary with sightseeing and a visit to the ancient ruins. She added a slow stroll through the affordable markets to taste the local food. Finally, Tuca looked at her plan and felt it was going to be unforgettable.
